decisions.title: Prioritize E2 GA readiness vs. GovCloud infrastructure investment
decisions.context: Heavy E2 feature work (annotations, candidate runs, family data DAG) alongside major GovCloud deployments (Windmill, Klu migration, FedRAMP sync). Resource allocation unclear between product GA and compliance infrastructure.

arguments.for: E2 represents core product innovation (Nail the Core pillar), directly impacts Decisions Captured metric. Feature flag audit signals imminent GA.
arguments.against: GovCloud enables government customer expansion (Build the Moat), represents significant ARR opportunity. Infrastructure work is foundational for both.

decisions.title: Resolve observability gaps before production load testing
decisions.context: ENG-10175 highlights ingest observability gaps. ENG-10193 improving alarm robustness. Testing production load without adequate monitoring risks blind spots in critical data pipeline.

arguments.for: Load testing validates scalability for larger customers (Embed Deeper). Delaying risks Q2 enterprise deals.
arguments.against: Testing without observability creates technical debt, potential production incidents. Better to invest 1-2 weeks in monitoring first.

decisions.title: Standardize on Bifrost LLM gateway vs. point solutions
decisions.context: ENG-10162 deploying Bifrost for internal tooling. Ask SIFT represents customer-facing AI. Need decision on centralized vs. distributed LLM architecture to avoid fragmentation.

arguments.for: Centralized gateway enables consistent cost controls, observability, prompt engineering. Supports Ask SIFT scalability.
arguments.against: Adds operational complexity. Point solutions may be faster for specific use cases. Gateway becomes single point of failure.

decisions.title: Accelerate CSRS SchemalessV1 adoption or maintain dual-write
decisions.context: ENG-9987 implementing SchemalessV1 reads, ENG-10157 adding deduplication. Migration path from legacy CSRS unclear. Dual systems increase operational burden.

arguments.for: Schemaless architecture critical for Canvas flexibility (Nail the Core). Faster iteration on data models. Deduplication improves data quality.
arguments.against: Migration risk for existing customers. Legacy CSRS battle-tested. Premature cutover could cause data loss incidents.

decisions.title: MFA enforcement timeline and rollout strategy
decisions.context: ENG-9300 requiring MFA for all users. Security critical but impacts user experience. Need phased rollout plan, customer communication, support readiness.

arguments.for: Table stakes for enterprise security (Build the Moat). Required for SOC 2, FedRAMP. Reduces account compromise risk.
arguments.against: Customer friction, especially for embedded workflows. Support tickets will spike. Need grace period and clear comms.

keyResults.name: E2 GA (family workflows, annotations, candidate runs)

timeAudit.weekSummary: High meeting load (80% scheduled time). E2 GA sprint driving cross-functional syncs. GovCloud workstream adding compliance reviews. Need to consolidate: 3 separate E2 standups, 2 architecture reviews covering overlapping scope.
alerts: Zero maker time scheduled Tuesday/Thursday—back-to-back meetings 9am-6pm
4 decision meetings without pre-reads or clear DRI identified
Platform team at 6 meetings/day average—above healthy threshold
